title: 三只瞎老鼠
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\151-三只瞎老鼠-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Molly Davis plays the nursery rhyme "Three Blind Mice" on the piano in a drawing room, following Chief Inspector Trotter's instructions to play softly. While playing, she experiences intense fear, sensing a trap and imagining Mr. Paravicini might be a threat. Chief Inspector Trotter enters the room, claiming he has achieved his purpose by identifying a liar. He accuses Molly of having first-hand knowledge of the Lorrach Farm case and reveals he knows her true identity as a former teacher at Abbyville School. Molly confesses that her sister was the teacher involved in a tragic incident where a child died after a letter for help went unread due to her sister's illness.

The situation turns violent when the man claiming to be Trotter reveals he is actually Jim, the brother of George, a victim from Lorrach Farm. Jim, acting with a childlike demeanor, holds Molly at gunpoint with a revolver stolen from her husband. He explains his motive is revenge against those who failed to help him and his brother during their abuse at the farm. As Jim threatens her, Metcalf Major leaps from behind a sofa and tackles him. The man revealed himself to be Detective Inspector Taner, who had been working undercover with Metcalf to monitor the situation.

After the assailant is subdued, the group prepares to leave. Molly reconciles with her husband, Jules, and they discover they had both traveled to London separately to buy each other anniversary gifts. Mr. Paravicini departs to join the police, promising Molly a mysterious gift of food and nylon stockings, while Christopher Raine notes an unpleasant smell coming from the kitchen, prompting Molly to realize her pie is ruined.
